subject: feat(scheduling): add server-side message scheduling
body:
Users can queue a message for future delivery via a clock-icon affordance
next to the send button. Survives app close / offline because dispatch runs
on the database via pg_cron.

Architecture:
  - New scheduled_messages table with RLS (sender-only), check constraints
    mirroring messages, partial index on (scheduled_for) for the hot path
  - dispatch_scheduled_messages() SECURITY DEFINER function + pg_cron
    '* * * * *' schedule moves due rows into messages; existing
    notify_new_message trigger handles pushes for free
  - Dispatcher handles: chat ended (failed/chat_ended), >1hr late
    (failed/too_stale), constraint violations (failed/sqlerrm)
  - check_rate_limit gains 'schedule' action type (10/hour)
  - services/scheduledMessages.ts: schedule/list/update/cancel,
    snapshots reply data at schedule time
  - useScheduledMessages hook with realtime + polling fallback
  - DateTimePicker wrapper: <input type="datetime-local"> on web,
    @react-native-community/datetimepicker on native
  - ScheduleComposerModal for picking send time, ScheduledMessagesModal
    for listing/rescheduling/cancelling
  - ChatHeaderMenu gets 'Scheduled (N)' entry on mobile; ChatPanel shows
    a compact pill on desktop
  - Tutorial gains a new 'Schedule Messages' step

Decisions locked in:
  - Cancel = hard delete (matches user mental model)
  - Modal-only, no ghost bubbles (preserves sender/receiver parity)
  - Native pickers per platform, wrapped in one component
  - 2min minimum / 30d maximum lead time, 5-min picker snap
  - 20 pending per user cap, 10 schedules/hour rate limit

Tags: scheduling, pg-cron, database, ui, tones, rate-limit
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: neutral
Epic: message-scheduling
Semver: minor
title: add server-side message scheduling
